title: Diet and Acne Systematic Review
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2ee29","text":"This protocol describes the planned methods for a systematic review investigating the association between dietary factors and acne vulgaris. The review will evaluate randomized controlled trials and observational studies that examine the effects of dietary exposures—including glycemic load, dairy, chocolate, omega-3 fatty acids, and overall dietary patterns—on acne incidence and severity.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bedu2","text":"The expected outcome of this protocol is a transparent, reproducible methodology that will synthesize existing evidence and clarify dietary contributions to acne development and progression. Results are anticipated to provide guidance for clinicians, patients, and researchers in understanding the role of nutrition in acne management.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"3t3am","text":"This systematic review follows the Preferred Reporting Items for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses (PRISMA 2020) guidelines. Study selection, data extraction, and synthesis are conducted in accordance with established methodological standards for systematic reviews.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3s2cl","text":"Limiting searches to English-only studies may introduce language bias.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c6dvk","text":"Reliance on self-reported dietary and acne severity data in included studies may increase risk of recall bias.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7ci5p","text":"Heterogeneity in study design and dietary assessment tools may limit generalizability of conclusions.n/a","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ik6","text":"Databases: PubMed (MEDLINE), Scopus, Embase, and Cochrane Library.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6ii8g","text":"Guidelines: PRISMA 2020 Statement and Checklist for systematic reviews.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"esrqb","text":"Reference Management: EndNote / Zotero / Mendeley (used for citation management and duplicate removal).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":21}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bmctn","text":"Screening Tools: Microsoft Excel / Covidence / Rayyan (used to track study inclusion/exclusion).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":16}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6luos","text":"Data Extraction Tools: Microsoft Excel or Google Sheets (for structured data collection, summary tables, and quality assessment).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":22}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dj2fp","text":"Figures: PRISMA 2020 Flow Diagram template (to illustrate study selection).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"epmb2","text":"Quality Assessment Tools: Cochrane Risk of Bias Tool (for randomized controlled trials) and Newcastle-Ottawa Scale (for observational studies).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Specify the research question: “ What is the relationship between dietary factors (glycemic load, dairy intake, sugar/fat consumption) and acne vulgaris? ”
Define Population, Intervention/Exposure, Comparator, and Outcomes (PICO framework).
Select databases: PubMed (MEDLINE), Scopus, Embase, and Cochrane Library.
Export all search results and remove duplicates.
Randomized controlled trials, cohort studies, case-control studies, cross-sectional studies
Studies focused solely on supplements or non-dietary interventions
Title and abstract screening by reviewers.
Full-text review of potentially eligible articles.
Record numbers at each stage in a PRISMA flow diagram.
Author, year, country, design, sample size, population
Enter data into a unified summary table (Supplemental Material).
Assess study quality using appropriate tools (e.g., Cochrane Risk of Bias for RCTs, Newcastle-Ottawa Scale for observational studies).
Record bias/limitations in data extraction sheet.
Summarize findings qualitatively across included studies.
Report effect sizes/meta-analyses from published literature where available (no new meta-analysis performed).
Present study selection process in a PRISMA flowchart.
Discuss strengths, limitations, and implications for dietary guidance in acne management.
creator: taliajaynethomas
